name: Bash
output:
<!doctype html>
<html lang="es">
<head>
  <meta charset="utf-8">
  <meta name="viewport" content="width=device-width, initial-scale=1">
  <title>HLG Cycling Academy - Promo Fundadores</title>
  <style>
    @import url("https://fonts.googleapis.com/css2?family=Bebas+Neue&family=Montserrat:wght@400;600;700;800;900&family=Playfair+Display:ital@1&display=swap");

    :root {
      color-scheme: dark;
      --hlg-red: #cc0000;
      --hlg-black: #111111;
      --hlg-white: #ffffff;
      --bg: #080808;
      --fg: #ffffff;
      --muted: #c8c8c8;
      --border: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.18);
      --accent: var(--hlg-red);
      --font-display: "Bebas Neue", "Arial Narrow", Impact, sans-serif;
      --font-body: "Montserrat", -apple-system, BlinkMacSystemFont, "Segoe UI", system-ui, sans-serif;
      --font-emphasis: "Playfair Display", Georgia, serif;
      --shadow-red: 0 0 24px rgba(204, 0, 0, 0.32);
    }

    * {
      box-sizing: border-box;
    }

    html,
    body {
      margin: 0;
      min-height: 100%;
      background: #050505;
      color: var(--fg);
      font-family: var(--font-body);
    }

    body {
      display: grid;
      place-items: center;
      padding: 24px;
    }

    .poster-wrap {
      position: relative;
      width: min(100vw - 48px, 540px);
      aspect-ratio: 4 / 5;
    }

    .post {
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0 auto auto 0;
      width: 1080px;
      height: 1350px;
      overflow: hidden;
      background: var(--bg);
      box-shadow: 0 32px 90px rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.62);
      isolation: isolate;
      transform-origin: top left;
    }

    .post::before {
      content: "";
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0;
      z-index: -3;
      background:
        linear-gradient(180deg, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.18) 0%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.52) 42%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.92) 100%),
        linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.78) 0%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.34) 52%, rgba(0, 0, 0, 0.72) 100%),
        url("assets/source-posters/founder-early-bird-cinematic.jpg") center / cover no-repeat;
      filter: contrast(1.1) saturate(0.92) brightness(0.84);
      transform: scale(1.02);
    }

    .post::after {
      content: "";
      position: absolute;
      inset: 0;
      z-index: -2;
      background:
        radial-gradient(circle at 14% 10%, rgba(204, 0, 0, 0.34), transparent 24%),
        linear-gradient(0deg, rgba(204, 0, 0, 0.18), transparent 36%);
      mix-blend-mode: screen;
      opacity: 0.72;
      pointer-events: none;
    }

    .safe {
      position: absolute;
      inset: 6.6%;
      display: grid;
      grid-template-rows: auto 1fr auto;
    }

    .topbar {
      display: flex;
      align-items: flex-start;
      justify-content: space-between;
      gap: 24px;
    }

    .badge {
      display: inline-flex;
      align-items: center;
      border: 1px solid var(--accent);
      border-radius: 6px;
      background: rgba(204, 0, 0, 0.16);
      color: var(--hlg-white);
      box-shadow: var(--shadow-red);
      padding: 9px 12px 8px;
      font-size: 14px;
      font-weight: 900;
      letter-spacing: 0.14em;
      line-height: 1;
      text-transform: uppercase;
    }

    .brand {
      display: grid;
      gap: 2px;
      text-align: right;
      color: var(--hlg-white);
      text-transform: uppercase;
    }

    .brand strong {
      font-family: var(--font-display);
      font-size: 66px;
      font-weight: 400;
      letter-spacing: 0.02em;
      line-height: 0.86;
    }

    .brand span {
      font-size: 13px;
      font-weight: 800;
      letter-spacing: 0.22em;
      color: rgba(255, 255, 255, 0.82);
    }

    .main {
      align-self: end;
      display: grid;
      gap: clamp(14px, 2.4vw, 26px);
      max-width: 88%;
      padding-bottom: clamp(16px, 3vw, 36px);
    }

    .eyebrow {
      width: fit-content;
      max-width: 690px;
      display: grid;
      gap: 8px;
      padding: 16px 20px 15px;
      color: var(--hlg-white);
      background: linear-gradient(90deg, rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.92), rgba(17, 17, 17, 0.62));
      border-top: var(--line-red);
      border-radius: 10px;

name: Bash
output:
---
name: "imagegen"
description: "Generate or edit raster images when the task benefits from AI-created bitmap visuals such as photos, illustrations, textures, sprites, mockups, or transparent-background cutouts. Use when Codex should create a brand-new image, transform an existing image, or derive visual variants from references, and the output should be a bitmap asset rather than repo-native code or vector. Do not use when the task is better handled by editing existing SVG/vector/code-native assets, extending an established icon or logo system, or building the visual directly in HTML/CSS/canvas."
---

# Image Generation Skill

Generates or edits images for the current project (for example website assets, game assets, UI mockups, product mockups, wireframes, logo design, photorealistic images, or infographics).

## Top-level modes and rules

This skill has exactly two top-level modes:

- **Default built-in tool mode (preferred):** built-in `image_gen` tool for normal image generation, editing, and simple transparent-image requests. Does not require `OPENAI_API_KEY`.
- **Fallback CLI mode:** `scripts/image_gen.py` CLI. Use when the user explicitly asks for the CLI/API/model path, or after the user explicitly confirms a true model-native transparency fallback with `gpt-image-1.5`. Requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`.

Within CLI fallback, the CLI exposes three subcommands:

- `generate`
- `edit`
- `generate-batch`

Rules:
- Use the built-in `image_gen` tool by default for normal image generation and editing requests.
- Do not switch to CLI fallback for ordinary quality, size, or file-path control.
- If the user explicitly asks for a transparent image/background, stay on built-in `image_gen` first: prompt for a flat removable chroma-key background, then remove it locally with the installed helper at `$CODEX_HOME/skills/.system/imagegen/scripts/remove_chroma_key.py`.
- Never silently switch from built-in `image_gen` or CLI `gpt-image-2` to CLI `gpt-image-1.5`. Treat this as a model/path downgrade and ask the user before doing it, unless the user has already explicitly requested `gpt-image-1.5`, `scripts/image_gen.py`, or CLI fallback.
- If a transparent request appears too complex for clean chroma-key removal, asks for true/native transparency, or local removal fails validation, explain that true transparency requires CLI `gpt-image-1.5 --background transparent --output-format png` because `gpt-image-2` does not support `background=transparent`, then ask whether to proceed. Run the CLI fallback only after the user confirms.
- The word `batch` by itself does not mean CLI fallback. If the user asks for many assets or says to batch-generate assets without explicitly asking for CLI/API/model controls, stay on the built-in path and issue one built-in call per requested asset or variant.
- If the built-in tool fails or is unavailable, tell the user the CLI fallback exists and that it requires `OPENAI_API_KEY`. Proceed only if the user explicitly asks for that fallback.
- If the user explicitly asks for CLI mode, use the bundled `scripts/image_gen.py` workflow. Do not create one-off SDK runners.
- Never modify `scripts/image_gen.py`. If something is missing, ask the user before doing anything else.

Built-in save-path policy:
- In built-in tool mode, Codex saves generated images under `$CODEX_HOME/*` by default.
- Do not describe or rely on OS temp as the default built-in destination.
- Do not describe or rely on a destination-path argument (if any) on the built-in `image_gen` tool. If a specific location is needed, generate first and then move or copy the selected output from `$CODEX_HOME/generated_images/...`.
- Save-path precedence in built-in mode:
  1. If the user names a destination, move or copy the selected output there.
  2. If the image is meant for the current project, move or copy the final selected image into the workspace before finishing.
  3. If the image is only for preview or brainstorming, render it inline; the underlying file can remain at the default `$CODEX_HOME/*` path.
- Never leave a project-referenced asset only a

content:
Voy a reemplazar el fondo como prioridad: menos oscuro, más propio para esta pieza y sin reutilizar el mismo arte base. Revisaré los assets disponibles y, si no hay una foto adecuada, generaré un fondo nuevo compatible con HLG.Encontré cuatro assets fuente y el fondo actual es `founder-early-bird-cinematic.jpg`. Para evitar repetir otro arte, voy a crear una composición nueva dentro del HTML: fondo más claro, con escena MTB estilizada y espacio limpio para texto, manteniendo la foto original fuera del poster.Haré el nuevo fondo como una escena propia, no como JPG reutilizado: montaña/route line/rider en silueta, con luz gris fría y una franja inferior oscura para proteger el texto. También bajaré la opacidad del overlay para que ya no se sienta tan apagado.
El fondo ya no depende del JPG anterior: ahora es una escena nueva dentro del arte, más luminosa arriba y protegida abajo. Voy a verificar que el HTML siga limpio, que no quede referencia al asset viejo y que el canvas conserve 1080×1350.La verificación estática confirma que ya no queda la foto anterior como fondo y el tamaño del canvas sigue correcto. Intentaré una revisión visual local ahora para detectar si el nuevo rider o la franja de protección se cruzan con el texto.
No hay una herramienta de navegador expuesta en esta sesión. Haré una comprobación alternativa con utilidades locales disponibles y, si no hay renderizador HTML, cerraré con la verificación de rutas/estructura.Actualicé [hlg-fundadores-instagram.html](${HOME}/Library/Application%20Support/Open%20Design/namespaces/release-stable/data/projects/ds-eres-el-disenador-oficial-design-system/hlg-fundadores-instagram.html).

Cambios principales:
- Quité la foto de fondo anterior.
- Creé un fondo nuevo para este arte: montaña, ruta y rider en silueta.
- Aclaré la parte superior para que no se vea tan oscuro.
- Mantengo una protección oscura abajo para que el texto, precio y CTA sigan leyendo bien.
- El canvas sigue en formato real `1080×1350`.

No pude hacer screenshot renderizado porque no hay navegador/renderizador disponible en esta sesión, pero verifiqué que ya no queda referencia al fondo anterior y que el HTML conserva el formato correcto.
